svg.text: Azure vWAN 3-Hub — Gateway-Learned Route Override Lab
VPN gateway-learned routes override Remote Hub routes when on-prem re-advertises Azure spoke prefixes
✗ Hub1 ↔ Hub2 Remote Hub path OVERRIDDEN by VPN gateway-learned routes
⚠ Hub Routing Preference set to VpnGateway (Hub1 & Hub2)
VpnGateway precedence: VPN Gateway > ExpressRoute > Remote Hub
Default (ExpressRoute): ER > VPN GW > Remote Hub | AS Path mode: shortest path wins
3 IPsec tunnels → VPN GW Instance 0 (each hub)
Strips Azure VPN GW ASN → bypasses BGP loop detection
STANDARD_OUT: permit ONPREM only, deny all else
3 IPsec tunnels → VPN GW Instance 1 (each hub)
Strips Azure VPN GW ASN → bypasses BGP loop detection
STANDARD_OUT: permit ONPREM only, deny all else
← Hub1 spoke routes re-advertised to Hub2 (and vice versa) →
Route Flow — How the Override Happens
1. Hub1 VPN GW sends spoke1/spoke2 routes to FRR via BGP → 2. FRR learns them (AS-path: 65515)
3. FRR strips 65515 and re-advertises to Hub2 VPN GW → 4. Hub2 accepts (no loop detected)
5. Hub2 now has VPN GW path to Hub1 spokes → VPN wins over Remote Hub (same in reverse)
